note: Implementer (Pump) example for OPC UA - OpenUSD Bindings. The generic spec stays in the base document; this descriptor is the Pump Annex source of truth and matches the validated PumpDeviceIntegrationServer bindings and the base asset in Plant.usda. Browse paths are relative to the represented Pump Object; a connector normally discovers SourceNodeId directly via Server/OpenUSD/Representations and does not need these paths.

stage.rootLayerDigestNote: The server publishes RootLayerDigest = SHA-256 over the resolved root layer (a Twin-BOM content-integrity check). The demo digests the rootLayerIdentifier as a deterministic stand-in; a connector verifies it before composing the stage.

servedAssets.note: Optional OU-AssetDelivery capability: the server serves these USD layers through <Stage>.Assets (Part 5 FileType); a connector fetches + verifies + caches them and composes the stage locally, no external asset repo needed.

bindings.name: MassFlowSpin
bindings.note: Impeller spin: mass flow scaled to a rotateZ angle by the connector. SourceSemanticId is an ECLASS-style IRDI (volume flow rate) a connector can use to resolve the source across vendors.

bindings.name: BearingTempColor
bindings.note: Temperature mapped to a blue->red gradient by the connector's color mapping.

bindings.name: DiffPressureEmissive
bindings.note: Differential pressure mapped to an emissive glow (status light) by the connector's color mapping.

bindings.name: AlarmActiveVisibility
bindings.note: Supervision alarm ActiveState (cavitation or motor overheat) drives the status-light visibility token: 'inherited' when active, 'invisible' when clear. In a full A&C deployment the source is a Part 9 condition ActiveState; the demo exposes a Boolean AlarmActive variable the connector reads directly.

bindings.name: SpeedSetpointCommand
bindings.note: Opt-in control direction: a USD-side speed setpoint intent is written to the UA SpeedSetpoint Variable. The binding is declared (enabled=true) and marked Controllable, but a connector actuates it only when explicitly enabled (bridge --enable-commands) AND authorized — single-writer, fail-closed. 'enabled=true' means declared, NOT auto-actuated; the opt-in lives on the connector, not on the Enabled member (whose false value is a tombstone).

components.name: ImpellerComponent
components.note: 1:1 composition (§5.12): the pump is composed of an Impeller component Object with its own representation, mapped to a child prim.

components.name: BearingComponent
components.note: 1:1 composition (§5.12): the pump is composed of a Bearing component Object mapped to a child prim.

components.name: PumpsAggregation
components.note: 1..n composition (§5.12/§5.13): a ProductionLine aggregates its pumps as instanceable reference prims; pumps are added/removed at runtime (GeneralModelChange), reconciled by the connector.

components.name: RemotePumpComponent
components.note: Cross-server composition (§5.14): an OEM pump hosted on another server; the connector composes its reference prim and federates to drive its bindings.
